MLS PLAYER
DEVELOPMENT
GUIDELINES
AS OF AUGUST 18, 2025

TABLE OF CONTENTS

B. HOMEGROWN TERRITORIES 6

MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 2

OVERVIEW
These MLS Player Development Guidelines (these Guidelines) detail the provisions governing Player
Development Programs and MLS Homegrown Players. These Guidelines reference relevant sections of the
MLS Roster and Budget Guidelines (as amended and restated from time to time, the Roster and Budget
Guidelines), the MLS Club Commercial Guidelines (as amended and restated from time to time, the Club
Commercial Guidelines), and MLS NEXT Protocols (as amended and restated from time to time). Capitalized
terms not otherwise defined in these Guidelines have the meanings ascribed to them in the MLS Roster and
Budget Guidelines. Any section or part references made, refer to the relevant section or part of these
Guidelines, unless noted otherwise. Where these Guidelines and MLS NEXT Protocols conflict with each other,
the provisions of these Guidelines prevail.
These Guidelines are hereby incorporated in, and are deemed a part of, the League Rules.
The League reserves the right at any time to modify these Guidelines and to establish exceptions to these
Guidelines in its sole and absolute discretion.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 3

A. DEFINITIONS
Academy means a Player Development Program (defined below) established by an MLS Club within its
Homegrown Territory for elite youth soccer talent organized in competitive teams of various age groups. An
Academy is operated and controlled by the MLS Club, offering full-time programming to players registered with
the Academy.
Academy Commitment Letter has the meaning set out in Section 9.6.
Acknowledgement of Player Obligations Memo has the meaning set out in Section 4.1.3.
Claim has the meaning set out in Section 16.1.
Claimant Club has the meaning set out in Section 16.2.
Current Club means the MLS Club with which a Recruitable Academy Player is currently registered in
accordance with Section 9.
Decision has the meaning set out in Section 20.
First MLS Club has the meaning set out in Section 9.8.
Homegrown Exclusivity has the meaning set out in Section 5.2 and Section 8.
Homegrown Information Sheet means an information sheet that summarizes the current Homegrown Player
criteria and requirements. It must be distributed by an MLS Club to its Academy participants annually.
Homegrown Player List or Homegrown List has the meaning set out in Sections 5.3 and Section 10.
Homegrown Player List Submission Dates or Submission Dates has the meaning set out in Section 13.2.
Homegrown Player means a Player signed to an MLS Standard Player Agreement who meets the requirements
to be assigned directly to an MLS Club as set forth in Section 7 without being subject to an alternative priority
mechanism.
Homegrown Player Request Form means the official form to be completed and submitted by an MLS Club to
sign a player as a Homegrown Player.
Homegrown Priority has the meaning set out in Section 5.1 and Section 6.
Homegrown Territory means an assigned geographic area for which an MLS Club has certain exclusive rights
to develop youth soccer talent.
Homegrown Territory Program means a type of Player Development Program set out in Section 3.1.
Mandatory Release Trial Windows has the meaning set out in Section 9.5.1.
MLS NEXT Scholarship means a program that establishes a pool of funds intended to provide support for the
opportunity for a player who currently plays for an MLS NEXT youth club outside of the MLS Academy system
to receive elite player development at an MLS Club’s Academy within the relevant Homegrown Territory (e.g.,
Dallas MLS NEXT club to FC Dallas Academy).
MLS NEXT Season means the period commencing on the date of the first MLS NEXT match fixture and ending
on the date of the last MLS NEXT match fixture.
MLS Standard Player Agreement or MLS SPA means the form Standard Player Agreement that all players must
sign to be under contract with MLS. For the purpose of these Guidelines, the term does not include a Short
Term SPA, as detailed under Article 18.8 of the 2020-2028 CBA.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 4

Non-Homegrown Territory Player Development Program means a type of Player Development Program set out
at Section 3.2.
Non-Homegrown Territory Player means a player who is not from and does not reside in any MLS Club’s
Homegrown Territory.
Official Notification means the written notice of interest set out in Section 9.4.
Player Development Program has the meaning set out in Section 3.
Plus 5 Player or Plus 5 has the meaning set out in Section 8.2.
Post-Formation Player has the meaning set out in Section 8.3.
Recruitable Academy Player is a type of Recruitable Player who is a Registered Academy Player and therefore
may be recruited by a different MLS Club to join its Academy in accordance with Section 9.3.
Recruitable Player means a player over whom no MLS Club retains a ROFR or Homegrown Exclusivity, based on
the Homegrown Player List, and therefore is eligible to be recruited by any MLS Club to register with its
Academy as set out in Section 9.1.
Recruiting Club means the MLS Club interested in a Recruitable Academy Player in accordance with Section 9.
Registered Academy Player or Academy Player is a player registered full-time with an MLS Club’s Academy and
whose participation in MLS NEXT or a similar youth soccer competition is limited to the competitive teams in
the MLS Club’s Academy.
Respondent Club has the meaning set out in Section 16.2.
Response has the meaning set out in Section 16.3.
Right of First Refusal or a ROFR – has the meaning set out in the Roster and Budget Guidelines.
Second MLS Club has the meaning set out in Section 9.8.
Statute of Limitations has the meaning set out in Section 16.5.
Tampering has the meaning set out in the Roster and Budget Guidelines. In the context of these Guidelines,
Tampering includes any conduct or attempted conduct, whether direct or indirect, to sign, negotiate with, make
an offer to, or influence an Underage Player or a player (or his representative) on another MLS Club’s
Homegrown List or in a different MLS Club’s Academy without first receiving written consent from the player’s
Current Club or the League.
The 35 Registered Academy Players or The 35 has the meaning set out in Section 8.1.
Trialist means a Recruitable Player who is participating in a trial with an MLS Club’s Academy.
Underage Player has the meaning set out in Section 9.2.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 5

B. HOMEGROWN TERRITORIES
1. HOMEGROWN TERRITORIES
The Homegrown Territory for each MLS Club is detailed below. The League may modify Homegrown
Territories from time to time (including, but not limited to, as a result of expansion).
Commentary: An MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory is not always identical to its Home Territory under the Club’s Operating
Agreement (as defined in the Club Commercial Guidelines). In many instances, an MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory is
significantly larger than its Home Territory. An MLS Club does not have any commercial and/or marketing rights in any part of
a Homegrown Territory that exceeds its Home Territory (e.g., use of Club Marks (as defined in the Club Commercial Guidelines),
sale of local commercial affiliations, license of local media rights, solicitation and/or sale of match tickets).
MLS CLUB HOMEGROWN TERRITORY
Atlanta United FC The state of Georgia and the following counties located in Tennessee: Bradley, Hamilton,
Marion, Polk, Sequatchie.
Austin FC The following counties located in Texas: Aransas, Atascosa, Bastrop, Bee, Bell, Bexar, Blanco,
Bosque, Burnet, Caldwell, Comal, Coryell, DeWitt, Falls, Fayette, Gillespie, Goliad, Gonzales,
Guadalupe, Hamilton, Hays, Karnes, Kendall, Kerr, Kleberg, Lampasas, Lavaca, Lee, Llano,
Mason, McLennan, Medina, Milam, Mills, Nueces, Refugio, San Patricio, San Saba, Travis,
Victoria, Williamson, Wilson
Charlotte FC The states of North Carolina and South Carolina
Chicago Fire FC A 75-mile radius of the Club’s stadium, including Milwaukee County.
Colorado Rapids The states of Colorado and New Mexico, excluding El Paso.
Columbus Crew The following counties located in Ohio, some of which may be shared with FC Cincinnati:
Allen, Ashland, Ashtabula, Athens, Auglaize, Belmont, Carroll, Champaign, Clark,
Columbiana, Coshocton, Crawford, Cuyahoga, Defiance, Delaware, Erie, Fairfield, Fayette,
Franklin, Fulton, Gallia, Geauga, Guernsey, Hancock, Hardin, Harrison, Henry, Hocking,
Holmes, Huron, Jackson, Jefferson, Knox, Lake, Lawrence, Licking, Logan, Lorain, Lucas,
Madison, Mahoning, Marion, Medina, Meigs, Mercer, Monroe, Morgan, Morrow, Muskingum,
Noble, Ottawa, Paulding, Perry, Pickaway, Pike, Portage, Putnam, Richland, Ross, Sandusky,
Scioto, Seneca, Stark, Summit, Trumbull, Tuscarawas, Union, Van Wert, Vinton, Washington,
Wayne, Williams, Wood, Wyandot
D.C. United A 75-mile radius of the Club’s stadium.
FC Cincinnati The following counties located in Ohio, some of which may be shared with Columbus Crew:
Adams, Allen, Ashtabula, Athens, Auglaize, Belmont, Brown, Butler, Carroll, Clermont,
Clinton, Columbiana, Cuyahoga, Darke, Defiance, Erie, Fulton, Gallia, Geauga, Greene,
Guernsey, Hamilton, Hancock, Harrison, Henry, Highland, Hocking, Huron, Jackson,
Jefferson, Lake, Lawrence, Lorain, Lucas, Mahoning, Medina, Meigs, Mercer, Miami, Monroe,
Montgomery, Morgan, Muskingum, Noble, Ottawa, Paulding, Perry, Pike, Portage, Preble,
Putnam, Ross, Sandusky, Scioto, Seneca, Shelby, Trumbull. Van Wert, Vinton, Warren,
Washington, Wayne, Williams, Wood.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 6

MLS CLUB HOMEGROWN TERRITORY
The following counties located in Indiana: Clark, Dearborn, Decatur, Fayette, Franklin, Henry,
Jefferson, Jennings, Ohio, Randolph, Ripley, Rush, Scott, Switzerland, Union, Wayne
The following counties located in Kentucky: Bath, Boone, Bourbon, Bracken, Campbell,
Carroll, Clark, Fayette, Fleming, Franklin, Gallatin, Grant, Harrison, Henry, Kenton, Lewis,
Mason, Montgomery, Nicholas, Oldham, Owen, Pendleton, Robertson, Scott, Shelby, Trimble,
Woodford
FC Dallas A 75-mile radius of the Club’s stadium, and all of Texas north of Dallas and Fort Worth.
Houston Dynamo The following counties located in Texas: Austin, Brazoria, Brazos, Burleson, Calhoun,
Chambers, Colorado, Fort Bend, Galveston, Grimes, Hardin, Harris, Houston, Jackson,
Jasper, Jefferson, Liberty, Madison, Matagorda, Montgomery, Newton, Orange, Polk, San
Jacinto, Trinity, Tyler, Walker, Waller, Washington, Wharton
Inter Miami CF A 100-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium (assuming Melreese site), excluding any non-
Floridian territories (i.e., the Bahamas).
Los Angeles FC A 75-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, excluding San Diego County
Los Angeles Galaxy A 75-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, excluding San Diego County.
Minnesota United FC The states of Minnesota and Wisconsin, excluding a 75-mile radius of Chicago Fire’s stadium,
but including Milwaukee County.
CF Montreal All of Canada, excluding: (i) Provinces of British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, and
Manitoba; and (ii) a 50-mile radius of Toronto FC’s stadium.
Nashville SC The state of Tennessee excluding the following counties: Bradley, Hamilton, Marion, Polk,
Sequatchie and including the following counties in Alabama: Limestone, Madison, Marshall,
Morgan.
New York City FC A 75-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, excluding a 25-mile radius of Philadelphia
Union’s home stadium.
New York Red Bulls A 75-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, excluding a 25-mile radius of Philadelphia
Union’s home stadium.
New England Revolution A 75-mile radius of the Club’s stadium, plus the state of Rhode Island.
Orlando City SC A 100-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, plus to the north of the stadium, the remainder
of the state of Florida.
Philadelphia Union A 75-mile radius of the Club’s home stadium, excluding: (i) the state of Maryland; and (ii) a 25-
mile radius of New York Red Bulls’ home stadium.
Portland Timbers The states of Idaho, Oregon, and New Mexico (excluding El Paso), and including Vancouver,
Washington, and its environs.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 7

MLS CLUB HOMEGROWN TERRITORY
Real Salt Lake The states of Utah and Arizona.
San Diego FC In the United States, a 75-mile radius of the Club’s training facility (assuming El Cajon site),
excluding Orange County, CA
In Mexico, a 62.1-mile (100-km) driving distance of the Club’s training facility (assuming El
Cajon site)
San Jose Earthquakes A 75-mile radius of the Club’s stadium.
Seattle Sounders FC The states of Hawaii and Washington, excluding a 50-mile radius from Portland Timbers’
home stadium.
Sporting Kansas City The states of Kansas and Oklahoma.
The state of Missouri, some of which might be shared with St. Louis City, excluding the
following counties: Ralls, Audrain, Pike, Lincoln, Montgomery, Callaway, Warren, St Charles,
St Louis (including the City of St Louis), Jefferson, Franklin, Gasconade, Osage, Maries,
Phelps, Crawford, Washington, St Francois, Saint Genevieve, Perry, Dent, Iron, Madison,
Cape Girardeua, Bollinger, Wayne, Reynolds, Scott, Stoddard, Butler, Mississippi, New
Madrid, Dunklin, Pemiscot.
The following counties in the state of Arkansas: Crawford, Madison, Carroll, Washington, and
Benton.
St. Louis City The state of Arkansas, excluding the following counties: Crawford, Madison, Carroll,
Washington, and Benton
The state of Missouri, some of which might be shared with Sporting KC, excluding the
following counties: Atchison, Nodaway, Holt, Worth, Harrison, Gentry, Daviess, Andrew,
DeKalb, Buchanan, Clinton, Caldwell, Livingston, Carroll, Ray, Clay, Platte, Jackson,
Lafayette, Johnson, Cass, Bates, Henry, St Clair, Vernon
The following counties located in Illinois: Adams, Schuyler, Fulton, Peoria, Tazewell, McLean,
Champaign, Edgar, plus to the south of these counties, the remainder of Illinois
The following counties located in Indiana: Posey, Vanderburg.
Toronto FC All of Canada, excluding the Provinces of British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan,
Manitoba, and Quebec.
Vancouver Whitecaps FC All of Canada, excluding: (i) a 50-mile radius of Toronto FC’s stadium; and (ii) the Province of
Quebec.
2. NON-HOMEGROWN TERRITORIES
Any area of the United States not considered a Homegrown Territory is a Non-Homegrown Territory. Player
Development Programs are limited to an MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory, except for Non-Homegrown
Territory Player Development Programs (as described in Section 3.2).
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 8

C. PLAYER DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMMING
3. PLAYER DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM
An MLS Club must establish a Player Development Program. There are two types of Player Development
Programs: (1) Homegrown Territory Player Programs and (2) Non-Homegrown Territory Player Programs.
3.1. Homegrown Territory Player Programs
3.1.1. Academy
An MLS Club may create an Academy within its Homegrown Territory for elite youth soccer talent to
develop their skills and to represent the MLS Club in youth soccer competition (e.g., MLS NEXT). An
MLS Club’s Academy must always comply with these Guidelines, MLS NEXT Protocols, and any related
League Rules.
In addition, if an MLS Club’s Academy offers a residency component, then the Academy must provide
and/or facilitate full-time education through high school.
3.1.2. Youth Affiliates
An MLS Club may form any number of youth affiliates with local amateur youth clubs within the MLS
Club’s Homegrown Territory that provide mutually agreed upon rights and benefits from MLS, an MLS
Club, and/or adidas. Before finalizing an affiliation, the MLS Club must seek authorization from the
League.
3.1.3. Other Programming
If an MLS Club offers other player development programming, including but not limited to talent
identification camps, local youth tournaments, and soccer clinics, then the MLS Club must hold the
programming within its Homegrown Territory.
3.2. Non-Homegrown Territory Player Programs
3.2.1. Up to Two Non-Homegrown Territory Player Development Programs
Subject to the League’s prior written approval, an MLS Club may form up to two (2) owned and
operated youth clubs outside of its Homegrown Territory, provided that the youth club:
a. is located within the United States or Canada;
b. is not located in another MLS Club’s Home Territory or Homegrown Territory; and
c. is fully owned and operated by the MLS Club (e.g., the coaches and training environment must be
under the direct supervision and control of the MLS Club).
Once a Non-Homegrown Territory Player Development Program is designated, the Club may only
change the designation to a different program after providing twenty-four (24) months written notice
to the League. The designation change is subject to the League’s written approval.
3.2.2. All Other Programs or Partnerships Are Not Permitted
Other than Non-Homegrown Territory Player Development Programs, an MLS Club must not form any
program or partnership outside its Homegrown Territory (regardless of whether it is for player
development, marketing, or branding purposes). To the extent these relationships exist, the League
may terminate these relationships. The League reserves the right to establish League Rules governing
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 9

such relationships.
Commentary: An MLS Club must not employ or contract any individual who is affiliated with a youth soccer program outside
its Homegrown Territory, as this would violate Section 3.2.2 above.
4. PLAYER DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS
If an MLS Club establishes an Academy, then the MLS Club must provide at least the following.
4.1. Rules and Regulations
4.1.1. Academy Player Handbook
If an MLS Club establishes an Academy, then the MLS Club must maintain a document that provides
information specific to the MLS Club’s Academy, including, but not limited to, Club contact
information, Club rules/policies, the Homegrown Information Sheet, the Academy Acknowledgment
Letter, and any other relevant information.
An MLS Club must distribute the Academy Player Handbook to players participating in the MLS Club’s
Academy annually.
4.1.2. FIFA Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players (“FIFA RSTP”)
An MLS Club must ensure that its Player Development Program complies with the FIFA RSTP,
including, but not limited to, Article 19 (“Protection of Minors”).
Please refer to Exhibit 5 for League guidance regarding MLS Club entitlement to Training
Compensation under the FIFA RSTP for players trained in its Academy.
4.1.3. Acknowledgment of Player Obligations Memo
An MLS Club must ensure that all Academy players and their parent(s) or legal guardian(s) review and
sign an acknowledgment form that provides information about the MLS Club’s Player Development
Program and its mission, details player obligations given the player’s affiliation with the MLS Club, and
explains that the MLS Club intends to preserve the player’s NCAA eligibility unless and until the player
agrees to a professional contract. The MLS Club must maintain a copy of each Academy player’s
signed Acknowledgement of Player Obligations Memo in its records.
Please refer to Exhibit 1 for the Acknowledgment of Player Obligations Memo.
4.1.4. Homegrown Information Sheet
An MLS Club must ensure that all Academy players and their parent(s) or legal guardian(s) review and
sign an information sheet summarizing the current Homegrown Player criteria and requirements. The
MLS Club must maintain a copy of each Academy player’s signed Homegrown Information Sheet in its
records.
Please refer to Exhibit 2 for the Homegrown Information Sheet.
4.1.5. NCAA Eligibility
An MLS Club must ensure that its Player Development Program does not jeopardize participating
players’ NCAA eligibility.
4.1.5. adidas Compliance
An MLS Club must ensure that all Academy coaches, staff, and players always wear adidas apparel
and footwear and use MLS match balls when representing their MLS Club (e.g., games, training, team
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 10

travel), except that players who have signed individual endorsement contracts with a different apparel
company may wear different apparel products during these activities. An MLS Club must ensure that
it manages its adidas product allotment so that adidas products are always available to all participating
coaches, staff, and players. Noncompliance is subject to League-imposed discipline, including fines.
4.1.6. MLS NEXT Rules and Regulations
An MLS Club participating in MLS NEXT must follow all MLS NEXT Protocols, including the MLS NEXT
Safety and Wellbeing Policy for MLS Academy players.
4.2. Training and Competition
4.2.1. MLS NEXT Seasons
An MLS Club must align its Player Development Program seasons with MLS NEXT Seasons.
4.2.2. Attendance Logs
An MLS Club must maintain attendance logs for each participating player in its Player Development
Program. The League may request these attendance logs, including those pertaining to training
sessions, at any time.
4.2.3. Youth Tournaments
An MLS Club must participate in youth soccer tournaments if instructed by the League (e.g.,
Generation adidas Cup).
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 11

D. HOMEGROWN PLAYERS: OVERVIEW AND HOMEGROWN PRIORITY
The rules governing prospective MLS Homegrown Players aim to incentivize MLS Clubs to develop top
domestic talent within MLS Academies, while accounting for variations across the geographies of the MLS
ecosystem.
5. HOMEGROWN FRAMEWORK
5.1. Homegrown Priority
Homegrown Priority is an entrance mechanism by which an MLS Club may sign an eligible player to a
professional contract and add the player directly to the MLS Club’s first-team roster with the
Homegrown Player designation.
5.2. Homegrown Exclusivity
Homegrown Exclusivity is an exclusive right held by an MLS Club to develop (without undue influence
from another MLS Club) for purposes of establishing Homegrown Priority up to forty (40) players
across three (3) age groups and certain former Registered Academy Players who have gone to college
or signed a professional contract with the MLS Club’s MLS NEXT Pro team.
Homegrown Exclusivity is not Bona Fide Consideration as defined in the Roster and Budget Guidelines.
5.3. Homegrown Player List
The Homegrown Player List is the mechanism by which an MLS Club formally documents its
Homegrown Exclusivity over qualifying players.
6. HOMEGROWN PRIORITY
An MLS Club establishes priority to sign a player to an MLS Standard Player Agreement (or an “MLS SPA”)
and to add the player to its first-team roster with the Homegrown Player designation after he completes
twelve (12) continuous months registered with an MLS Club’s Academy and/or an MLS Club’s MLS NEXT
Pro team as a Registered Academy Player. The end of the twelve (12)-month period must fall between
January 31 of the year in which the player turns fourteen (14) and the year in which he turns eighteen (18).
7. HOMEGROWN PLAYER
To add a player to an MLS Club’s first-team roster as a Homegrown Player, the MLS Club must comply
with the following:
a. Have established Homegrown Priority and maintained Homegrown Exclusivity;
b. Submit to the League a Homegrown Player Request Form via MLS Connect; and
c. Engage with the League Office before agreeing to any terms with a potential Homegrown Player
and/or his representative(s).
Upon receiving League approval, an MLS Club may add a Homegrown Player to its first-team roster.
Commentary: Upon receipt of the Homegrown Player Request Form, the League Office will determine, in its sole and absolute
discretion, whether the MLS Club has Homegrown Priority and Homegrown Exclusivity over the player. If approved by the
League, the Homegrown Player will not be subject to the MLS SuperDraft or any other central entrance mechanism. There is
no limit on the number of Homegrown Players an MLS Club may add to its first-team roster.
Commentary: For an MLS Club to achieve Homegrown Priority over a player by January 31 of the year the player turns fourteen
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 12

(14), the player must: (a) be a Registered Academy Player with the MLS Club on such date, and (b) have been registered with
the MLS Club for twelve (12) continuous months prior to January 31 of the year the player turns fourteen (14).
Commentary: An MLS Club may sign a player over whom the MLS Club has not yet achieved Homegrown Priority to an MLS
SPA with the intention of adding the player to its MLS first-team roster as a Homegrown Player. In this case, the MLS Club
would wait until the MLS Club achieves Homegrown Priority over the player before adding the player to the MLS Club’s first-
team roster (i.e., twelve continuous months registered with the MLS Club’s Academy and/or the MLS Club’s MLS NEXT Pro
team).
Commentary: An MLS Club may trade for Homegrown Priority for a player over whom a different MLS Club has achieved
Homegrown Priority. As a result of the trade, the player will be considered a Homegrown Player for the acquiring MLS Club if
he signs an MLS SPA.
Commentary: An MLS Club may trade Homegrown Priority over a player but not Homegrown Exclusivity over a player nor rights
to Underage Players within its Homegrown Territory. For example, if a player is listed on the MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List
but the MLS Club has not achieved Homegrown Priority over the player, then the MLS Club does not have Bona Fide
Consideration to trade related to the player. If the player wants to join a different MLS Club’s Academy, his current and future
MLS Clubs may agree to a settlement whereby, for example, the Current Club waives the right to bring a tampering charge or
agrees to remove the player from its Plus 5. MLS Clubs may negotiate a settlement among themselves and later submit the
settlement for League review and approval.
Commentary: The League may, in its sole and absolute discretion, continue to sign any top young player to Generation adidas
for the MLS SuperDraft irrespective of his placement on an MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 13

E. HOMEGROWN EXCLUSIVITY
With the intention of establishing Homegrown Priority, an MLS Club holds the exclusive right to develop
players who meet the qualifications listed below (without undue influence from another MLS Club). A player
over whom an MLS Club maintains Homegrown Exclusivity, by including the player on its Homegrown Player
List, is ineligible to be recruited by other MLS Clubs.
8. HOMEGROWN EXCLUSIVITY- ELIGIBILITY
8.1. “The 35” Registered Academy Players
An MLS Club may establish Homegrown Exclusivity over up to thirty-five (35) Registered Academy
Players between its U15, U17, and U19 age groups. For the U15 and U17 age groups respectively, an
MLS Club may include no more than twenty (20) players. For the U19 age group, an MLS Club may
include no more than fifteen (15) players.
8.1.1. Homegrown Exclusivity over a Registered Academy Player expires when the player departs the MLS
Club’s Academy.
a. Extending Homegrown Exclusivity over a Departing Registered Academy Player
An MLS Club may extend Homegrown Exclusivity over the departing Registered Academy Player by
including the player on its Homegrown Player List as a Plus 5 Player or Post-Formation Player, if
otherwise eligible.
For a former Registered Academy Player who is not eligible to be included on the Homegrown Player
List as a Post-Formation Player, an MLS Club may extend Homegrown Exclusivity over the player by
including the player on its Homegrown Player List as a Plus 5 Player only if the MLS Club has already
achieved Homegrown Priority over the player and the player meets the requirements of a Plus 5
Player except for 8.2.1.b.
b. Seeking a Right of First Refusal (ROFR) for a Departing Registered Academy Player
An MLS Club may seek a ROFR for the departing Registered Academy Player by requesting that the
League makes, on behalf of the MLS Club, a Genuine Offer to the player for an MLS SPA or for an
MLS NEXT Pro contract (as long as an MLS SPA is also offered and begins within twelve (12) months
of the start date of the MLS NEXT Pro contract) by August 31 of the year that he turns eighteen (18)
years old.
If the player declines the offer, then the MLS Club may submit a request for a ROFR through MLS
Connect.
Commentary: See Figure E(A) below.
Commentary: Further to the Roster and Budget Guidelines, a Club will only be eligible to receive a ROFR if the League issues a
written Genuine Offer to the player on behalf of the Club. Alternatively, the Club may issue the Genuine Offer only if the League
previously approved the Genuine Offer and if the League is copied on all Club correspondence.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 14

Commentary: Offering an MLS NEXT Pro contract alone does not provide an MLS Club the opportunity to be granted a ROFR.
The MLS Club must also offer an MLS SPA that would begin within twelve (12) months of the start date of the MLS NEXT Pro
contract.
8.2. “Plus 5” Players
8.2.1. Additionally, an MLS Club may establish Homegrown Exclusivity over up to five (5) players who:
a. Are not Registered Academy Players;
b. Have a permanent address in or currently reside with their parent(s) or legal guardian(s) in the MLS
Club’s Homegrown Territory;
c. Are in one of the U15, U17, or U19 age groups. For each age group, an MLS Club may include up to
five (5) players; and
d. Each of the five (5) players, the player’s family, and the player’s current associated soccer club have
been notified in writing by the MLS Club of the player’s inclusion as a Plus 5 player. In its written
notification, the Club must also offer a position in the Club’s Academy in the current season
corresponding with the applicable Submission Date.
8.2.2. Homegrown Exclusivity over a non-professional player Plus 5 player expires August 31 of the year the
player turns eighteen (18) or upon his enrollment in college (whichever is sooner); thereafter, the player is
no longer eligible for the Homegrown Player List.
8.2.3. Homegrown Exclusivity over a professional player listed on an MLS Club’s Plus 5 expires December 31 of
the year that he turns seventeen (17) years old; thereafter, the player is no longer eligible for the
Homegrown Player List. If the MLS Club does not sign the player to an MLS SPA or to a professional
contract in MLS NEXT Pro by that date, the player will be subject to priority rules as outlined in the Roster
and Budget Guidelines.
8.2.4. If an MLS Club requests that the League makes, on behalf of the MLS Club, a Genuine Offer to a Plus 5
player for an MLS SPA or for an MLS NEXT Pro contract (as long as an MLS SPA is also offered and begins
within twelve (12) months of the start date of the MLS NEXT Pro contract) by December 31 of the year
that he turns seventeen (17) years old and if the player declines, then the MLS Club may submit a request
for a ROFR through MLS Connect
Commentary: See Figure E(B) below.
Commentary: Offering an MLS NEXT Pro contract alone does not provide an MLS Club the opportunity to be granted a ROFR.
Commentary: The term priority in Section 8.2.3 refers to the concept of priority detailed in the Roster and Budget Guidelines
and not Homegrown Priority.
Commentary: An MLS Club must not include a Plus 5 Player on its Homegrown Player List who currently resides in a different
MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory or in a Non-Homegrown Territory.
8.3. Post-Formation Players: College and MLS NEXT Pro
8.3.1. An MLS Club may extend Homegrown Exclusivity, for a limited time, over any former Registered
Academy Player enrolled in college or signed to a professional contract with the MLS Club’s MLS NEXT
Pro team, if the MLS Club:
a. Already achieved Homegrown Priority over the player at the time of college enrollment or of signing
a professional contract in MLS NEXT Pro; and
b. Properly included the player on its Homegrown Player List at the time of college enrollment or of
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 15

signing a professional contract in MLS NEXT Pro.
8.3.2. If an MLS Club signs a player to a professional contract with MLS or the MLS Club’s MLS NEXT Pro team
who is otherwise eligible to be listed among its 35 Registered Academy Players, then the MLS Club may
list the player among its Post-Formation players.
(8.3.1.-8.3.2. together, Post-Formation Players)
8.3.3. For Post-Formation Players enrolled in college, Homegrown Exclusivity expires December 1 of the
player’s collegiate senior year (i.e., the player’s fourth (4th) year of enrollment), at which point an MLS Club
must offer an MLS SPA to sign the player as a Homegrown Player if the MLS Club wishes to remove the
player from the MLS SuperDraft.
If a player is enrolled in college, an MLS Club need not offer the player a professional contract before
December 1 of the player’s collegiate senior year. But if the player leaves college early to pursue
professional playing opportunities, then the MLS Club may request that the League makes, on behalf of
the MLS Club, a Genuine Offer to the player for an MLS SPA or for an MLS NEXT Pro contract (as long
as an MLS SPA is also offered and begins within twelve (12) months of the start date of the MLS NEXT
Pro contract). The MLS Club will not retain priority if an MLS SPA is not offered.
If the player declines the offer, then the MLS Club may submit a request for a ROFR through MLS
Connect.
8.3.4. For Post-Formation Players signed with an MLS Club’s MLS NEXT Pro team, Homegrown Exclusivity
expires on the earlier of the expiration date of the MLS NEXT Pro contract or December 1 of the year the
player turns twenty-two (22) years old.
Commentary: Section 8.3.2 addresses the scenario where an MLS Club recruits a player to join its Academy for the first
time by signing the player to a professional contract. Ordinarily, the player would register with the Academy and the
MLS Club would list the player among its 35 Registered Academy Players on the Homegrown Player List until the MLS
Club achieves Homegrown Priority over the player to add the player to the MLS Club’s first-team roster as a
Homegrown Player. With this Section, an MLS Club may choose to list the player among its Post-Formation Players for
this period rather than using a slot among The 35 to do so.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 16

8.4 FIGURE E(A)
8.5 FIGURE E(B)
9. RECRUITING
Subject to the rules below, an MLS Club may recruit eligible players to join its Academy. These rules only
apply to MLS Clubs and their Academies (i.e., MLS Clubs recruiting eligible players from other MLS Clubs).
MLS Clubs recruiting players from non-MLS MLS NEXT clubs must follow MLS NEXT Protocols.
9.1. Recruitable Players
If no MLS Club retains a ROFR or Homegrown Exclusivity over a player, based on the Homegrown
Player List, then the player is eligible to be recruited by any MLS Club to register with its Academy
subject to the below requirements (Recruitable Players).
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 17

9.2. Underage Players
9.2.1. Underage Players – Ineligible to be Recruited
If a player is (or turns) thirteen (13) years old or younger in the year of these Guidelines and has a
permanent address in or currently resides with their parent(s) or legal guardian(s) in an MLS Club’s
Homegrown Territory, then the player is not eligible to be recruited.
9.2.2. Shared Territories
MLS Clubs with overlapping Homegrown Territories may recruit and include in their respective Player
Development Programming a youth player within their Homegrown Territory who is thirteen (13) years
old or younger in the year of these Guidelines. Except that an MLS Club must not recruit a player who
is registered with the shared territory MLS Club’s Academy and competing within MLS NEXT
competition.
Commentary: An MLS Club may recruit an Underage Player who has a permanent address in or currently resides with their
parents(s) or legal guardian(s) in its own Homegrown Territory to join its Academy.
9.3. Remuneration for Recruitable Academy Players
9.3.1. If an MLS Club interested in recruiting a Recruitable Player (the Recruiting Club) wants to register a
Recruitable Academy Player (i.e., a type of Recruitable Player who is also a Registered Academy Player),
then the Recruiting Club must provide Official Notification to the MLS Club with which the player is
currently registered (the Current Club) (See Section 9.4 below). Upon the player registering with the
Recruiting Club, the Current Club may request that the League instruct the Recruiting Club to provide
to the Current Club the following:
a. $20,000 General Allocation Money payable upon the player signing an MLS SPA; and
b. Twenty Percent (20%) of any future transfer fee, loan fee, or training compensation received
(regardless of the MLS Club with which the player is registered at the time of the transfer, loan, or
registration as a professional) in the event the player transfers to a club outside MLS or of any
Financial Consideration received if the player is traded for Financial Consideration within MLS.
Note, remuneration under this Section does not include Homegrown Priority, regardless of whether it
has been achieved by the Current Club.
Notwithstanding the above, MLS Clubs may agree to different terms. If the MLS Clubs are unable to
reach an agreement, then the League will impose the preset terms above. The one (1) year Statute of
Limitations applies to all remuneration requests.
9.3.2. All other Recruitable Players may be recruited and subsequently registered to a Recruiting Club with no
renumeration immediately due to the player’s former club or academy, save for
any amounts owed in connection with the MLS NEXT Scholarship.
Commentary: The remuneration above is not applicable if the player remains a Recruitable Player upon the distribution of the
Homegrown Player List following the player’s departure from the Current Club.
9.4. Official Notification and Communication of Player Interest
9.4.1. For a Recruitable Academy Player, a Recruiting Club must provide a written notification of interest for
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 18

the player to his Current Club. The Recruiting Club must include the following information in the notice:
a. Player Name
b. Date of Birth
c. Request for Parent Contact Information
d. Request that the Club notify Parent(s) of the Recruiting Club’s interest
e. Whether trial is requested and on what basis
Commentary: If the Recruiting Club is interested in recruiting more than one Recruitable Academy Player from the same MLS
Club, then the Recruiting Club may aggregate its requests in one notification.
9.4.2. The Recruiting Club must not contact the Recruitable Academy Player or his family until it provides
Official Notification and receives a response from the Current Club, as outlined in Section 9.4.3 below.
The same rule applies to an MLS Club that receives unsolicited contact from a player who the MLS Club
is interested in recruiting.
9.4.3. The Current Club must notify the Recruitable Academy Player and his family of the Official Notification
and the request for trial (if any) within three (3) calendar days of receiving the Official Notification. Within
the same three (3)-day period, the Current Club must respond in writing to the Recruiting MLS Club with
the following:
a. Player Name
b. Date of Birth
c. Confirmation that the player and his family have been notified
d. Consent to contact the player and the contact details of the player’s family (alternatively, if the MLS
Club does not grant consent, the basis for the decision (including that the family supports the
decision))
e. Suggested dates for trial (including alternative dates), if applicable
Commentary: In the event the Current Club fails to respond in writing within three (3) days of receiving the Official Notification,
the Recruiting Club may request via email intervention from the League.
9.5. Player Trials
A Recruiting Club may invite a Recruitable Academy Player to trial with the MLS Club’s Academy.
Subject to the rules below, if the Recruitable Academy Player accepts an invitation to trial with the MLS
Club, then he and the Recruiting Club may together determine the details of the trial.
9.5.1. Mandatory Release Trial Windows
A Recruitable Academy Player may trial with an MLS Club’s Academy during either of the two
Mandatory Release Trial Windows below without consent of his current MLS Club. Although consent
from the Current Club is not required, the Recruiting Club and Current Club must comply with the
communication requirements above and are encouraged to collaborate to find trial dates that do not
interfere with schedules of the Recruitable Academy Player and the involved MLS Clubs.
• Spring Trial Window (February – March) – This window follows the Spring Homegrown Player
List Submission
• Fall Trial Window (September – December) – This window follows the Fall Homegrown Player
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 19

List Submission
MLS Clubs recruiting a Recruitable Academy Player may request a trial outside of the Mandatory
Release Trial Windows, but the Recruitable Player’s current MLS Club may decline to release the
Player.
9.5.2. Trial Structure Requirements
A Recruitable Academy Player on trial with an MLS Club’s Academy (a Trialist) may trial for up to seven
(7) days, so long as the trial does not unreasonably interfere with the Trialist’s youth competition
matches with the Current Club. The Recruiting Club must not include a Trialist in its official
competition matches (e.g., MLS NEXT).
9.6. Academy Commitment Letters
A Recruiting Club may enter a commitment letter with a Recruitable Player who intends to register with
the MLS Club’s Academy but wants to reserve a position in the Academy until the end of the current
school year, subject to the below (an Academy Commitment Letter). The Recruiting Club must return
a fully executed Academy Commitment Letter for League review and approval. Thereafter, the League
will notify all MLS Clubs about players who have signed Academy Commitment Letters for that year.
9.6.1. Academy Commitment Letters During Fall Trial Window
During the Fall Trial Window, a player may sign an Academy Commitment Letter of one (1) of two (2)
lengths with a Recruiting Club:
a. An Academy Commitment Letter to reserve the player’s registration with the Recruiting Club’s
Academy for the immediately following January 1; or
b. An Academy Commitment Letter to reserve the player’s registration with the Recruiting Club’s
Academy for the immediately following MLS NEXT Season.
9.6.2. Academy Commitment Letter for the Spring Trial Window
During the Spring Trial Window, a player may only sign an Academy Commitment Letter with a
Recruiting Club to reserve his registration with the Recruiting Club’s Academy for the immediately
following MLS NEXT Season.
9.7. MLS Club Obligations for Academy Commitment Letters
9.7.1. By signing a Recruitable Player to an MLS Academy Commitment Letter, an MLS Club’s Academy
must comply with the following:
a. Before or upon the next MLS NEXT Season or the immediate January 1, the MLS Club’s Academy
must register the player subject to MLS NEXT rules and regulations; and
b. Upon the next MLS NEXT Season or following the immediate January 1, the MLS Club must list the
player on its Homegrown Player List for at least the next two (2) Homegrown Player List Submission
Dates.
9.7.2. After the League approves a fully-signed MLS Academy Commitment Letter, the League will include the
player and his relevant information on a separate tab on the League’s Homegrown Player List, which will
be updated bi-monthly via MLS Connect. While the MLS Academy Commitment Letter remains
effective, the player is not eligible to be recruited by other MLS Clubs.
Commentary: For Section 9.7.1a, the MLS Club’s Academy must register the player within the immediately following MLS NEXT
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 20

Season. Therefore, an Academy Commitment Letter to reserve the player’s registration with the MLS Club’s Academy for the
immediately following January 1 is only applicable if the Academy Commitment Letter is signed during the fall MLS NEXT
Season (August through December).
9.8. All Other Player Relocations Must Be for Non-Soccer Reasons
If a player who relocates from the Homegrown Territory of one MLS Club (the First MLS Club) to the
Homegrown Territory of another MLS Club (the Second MLS Club) and if the Second MLS Club wants
to register the player to its Academy, then the Second MLS Club must request written confirmation
from the First MLS Club that the player relocated for non-soccer reasons and be prepared to
demonstrate that the player relocated for non-soccer reasons. This obligation applies to the following
categories of players:
a. A player who relocates as an Underage Player and is looking to register with an MLS Club’s Academy
for the first time
b. A player included on the First MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List - Plus 5 who relocates from the First
MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory
c. A Registered Academy Player, regardless of being listed among The 35 of the First MLS Club’s
Homegrown Player List
If the First MLS Club does not provide written confirmation, then the Second MLS Club may add the
player to its Academy and/or Homegrown Player List at its own risk.
Commentary: If the First MLS Club does not provide written confirmation that the player moved for non-soccer reasons, then
the Second MLS Club risks tampering sanctions for the player’s relocation. It is up to the Second MLS Club to conduct proper
due diligence to minimize this risk, as the League will presume that the player relocated for soccer reasons if the First MLS Club
files a claim. The Second MLS Club would have the burden to prove otherwise. MLS Clubs should note the Statute of
Limitations for filing claims at Section 16.5.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 21

F. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST
To include a player on its Homegrown Player List and thereby maintain Homegrown Exclusivity, an MLS Club
must comply with the applicable eligibility requirements below.
10. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST – ELIGIBLE PLAYERS
10.1. Components of the Homegrown Player List
An MLS Club must submit to the League its Homegrown Player List according to this Section. The
Homegrown Player List consists of players over whom an MLS Club retains Homegrown Exclusivity,
specifically, The 35, Plus 5, and Post-Formation Players, as outlined in the below chart.
10.2. Including a Player on the Homegrown Player List
To include a player on its Homegrown Player List, for the first time or as a re-addition, an MLS Club
must ensure the following:
a. The player has a birth year corresponding to one of the following age groups: U15, U17, and U19, as
determined by the year in which the applicable Homegrown Player List is submitted.
b. For The 35, the player became a Registered Academy Player by January 1 of the year he turns
eighteen (18) years old.
c. For the Plus 5, the player is included by the Fall Submission Date of the year he turns seventeen (17)
years old.
d. The MLS Club has accurately listed all the required player information as detailed in Exhibit 3.
FIGURE: F(A) - Homegrown Exclusivity Over Players on the Homegrown Player List

Commentary: If an MLS Club does not have a U19 team for Registered Academy Players, then the MLS Club may include
amateurs registered and competing full-time with its MLS NEXT Pro team among The 35 on its Homegrown Player List. In
addition, the MLS Club may include among its Plus 5 players who are not registered with the MLS Club but reside within its
Homegrown Territory, subject to eligibility requirements.
Commentary: An MLS Club must list a player based on birth year, not according to the competitive academy team for which
he may play.
Commentary: As detailed in Section 8.2.2., Homegrown Exclusivity over a Plus 5 player expires August 31 of the year the player
turns eighteen (18) or upon his enrollment in college (whichever is sooner); thereafter, the player is no longer eligible for the Plus
5.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 22

11. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST – INELIGIBLE PLAYERS
An MLS Club must not add (or re-add) the following players to its Homegrown List:
11.1. Underage Players
A player who is (or turns) thirteen (13) or younger in the year of these Guidelines (irrespective of whether
he is registered in the MLS Club’s Academy) is not eligible to be included on an MLS Club’s Homegrown
Player List.
11.2. A Player Already in College or in MLS NEXT Pro
A player who is enrolled in college or participating in MLS NEXT Pro as a professional is not eligible to
be included on an MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List, unless he otherwise qualifies and is properly
included as a Post-Formation Player pursuant to Section 8.3 above.
11.3. Players on Another MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List
A player is only eligible to be listed on one MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List. An MLS Club must not
include a player on its Homegrown Player List if he is currently listed on another MLS Club’s
Homegrown Player List.
Commentary: See Section 9.8. for requirements regarding player relocation from one MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory to
another MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory.
12. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST - SHARED TERRITORIES
With respect to MLS Clubs with shared Homegrown Territories:
12.1. Initial Listing on Plus 5
Notwithstanding Section 11.3, MLS Clubs that share Homegrown Territories may include the same Plus
5 Player on their respective Homegrown Player Lists if the player was initially included by both MLS
Clubs on the same Submission Date.
12.2. Registered Academy Players May Only Be Listed on One Homegrown Player List
In the event of Section 12.1 above, if the Plus 5 Player subsequently becomes a Registered Academy
Player with one of the MLS Clubs, then the other MLS Club(s) must remove the player from its next
Homegrown Player List.
12.3. Registered Academy Players Are Ineligible to Be Recruited
An MLS Club must not recruit a player within the shared Homegrown Territory if he is among The 35
Registered Academy Players of another MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List. For Underage Players
within a shared Homegrown Territory, refer to Section 9.2.2.
13. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST - SUBMISSIONS
13.1. Purpose of the Homegrown Player List Submission
With each Submission Date, an MLS Club informs the League (which will subsequently inform all other MLS
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 23

Clubs) of players over whom the MLS Club is asserting Homegrown Exclusivity
13.2. Two Submissions Per Year
An MLS Club has two opportunities over the course of a given year to submit to the League updates to
its Homegrown Player List:
a. Fall Submission Date; and
b. Spring Submission Date
(together, the Homegrown List Submission Dates or Submission Dates)
13.3. Complete and Accurate Submissions
As confirmed in the Homegrown Player List Certification Form (Exhibit 4), an MLS Club must maintain
a complete and accurate Homegrown Player List that is managed and submitted to the League
according to the requirements of this Section.
13.3.1. An MLS Club must comply with MLS NEXT Protocols, which coexist with the Submission Dates.
13.3.2. Figure F(B) below details a non-exhaustive list of the relevant MLS NEXT player movement dates and
their interaction with the Homegrown List Submission Dates.
14. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST – SUBMISSION DATES AND PLAYER MOVEMENT CALENDAR

G. COMPLIANCE
15. BREACH OF MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES
An MLS Club’s noncompliance with any of its obligations in these Guidelines may be considered a breach
of these Guidelines and/or League Rules. Further, the following non-exhaustive conduct is considered a
breach of these Guidelines, whether committed by the MLS Club or its MLS NEXT Pro team:
15.1.1 Recruit, communicate, trial, register, or move or attempt to recruit, communicate, trial, register, or move a
player on another MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List;
15.1.2. Recruit, communicate, trial, register, or move or attempt to recruit, communicate, trial, register, or move
an Underage Player from another MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory;
15.1.3. Engage in conduct or attempt to engage in conduct that violates MLS Tampering Rules;
15.1.4. Act in bad faith or dishonesty in managing or submitting its Homegrown Player List;
15.1.5. Submit a late, inaccurate, incomplete, or otherwise noncompliant Homegrown Player List; and
15.1.6. Engage in conduct that is intended to circumvent these Guidelines or obstruct the League’s
investigation into a potential breach of the Guidelines.
Commentary: Under MLS Tampering Rules, an MLS Club must not tamper with players over whom the MLS Clubs does not
have priority rights without first obtaining written permission. Therefore, an MLS Club must not tamper with a player on the
Homegrown Player List of another MLS Club or with Underage Players from another MLS Club’s Homegrown Territory. Further,
an MLS Club must not use its MLS NEXT Pro team to circumvent these Guidelines and/or League Rules. If a player or a player’s
family or representative initiates contact with an MLS Club and further contact between the player or player’s family or
representative and the MLS Club would violate the Guidelines and/or League Rules, then the MLS Club must immediately
notify the League before engaging further. Failure to do so is considered a breach of these Guidelines and/or League Rules.
16. CLAIMS
16.1. Filing a Claim
An MLS Club may file a dispute via MLS Connect with the League against another MLS Club regarding
a potential breach of these Guidelines (a Claim). An MLS Club filing a Claim must commit to paying a
bond of Five Thousand And No/100 Dollars (USD $ 5,000.00) following a League Decision that does
not find that the Claim successfully shows that the Respondent Club breached these Guidelines.
16.2.Claim Requirements
Notwithstanding the mandatory fields and instructions detailed in the Claim form via MLS Connect,
the Claim must include:
a. Name of MLS Club submitting the Claim (a Claimant Club),
b. Name of MLS Club against which the Claim is made (a Respondent Club)
c. Player’s full name
d. Player’s date of birth
e. All player home addresses and dates of residence relevant to the Claim
f. Home address of player’s Parent(s) or Legal Guardian(s) and dates of residence (if different from
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 25

above) relevant to the claim
g. Exact duration Player was registered in Academy
h. Reason(s) the Claimant Club is submitting the Claim
i. Factual information supporting the Claim
j. Evidence supporting the Claim, which must be attached, properly labeled, and comprise, where
applicable:
(i) At least one piece of corroborating evidence confirming the player’s home address;
(ii) Proof of the player’s registration; and/or
(iii) Any relevant information or correspondence regarding the player/family or the Respondent Club
k. Confirmation that the bond payment of Five Thousand And No/00 Dollars (USD $ 5,000.00) will be
posted if and when directed by the League.
Commentary: The purpose of the bond is to ensure that an MLS Club does not file a frivolous claim. A Claim must be ripe, and
the facts substantiated, otherwise the Claimant Club risks paying its full bond.
Commentary: Upon receiving the League’s instructions, a Claimant Club must pay the bond to MLS by wire transfer in US
dollars (USD) to the bank account details.
Commentary: Failure to meet the requirements above will result in an incomplete claim, which will not be reviewed by the
League and, therefore, the Claimant Club will pay its full bond. Save for technical malfunctions, A Claimant Club will not have
the opportunity to re-submit its claim; therefore, the Claimant Club should be overinclusive of any information or evidence it
deems relevant to its Claim.
16.3. Response
16.3.1.F ollowing receipt of the Claim, the Respondent Club must respond within seven (7) days to the League
via MLS Connect (a Response), which must state whether it accepts or denies each fact alleged.
16.3.2. Where a material fact of the Claim is denied, the Respondent Club must provide a summary of any
defense and evidence supporting its defense in accordance with Section 16.2.j above.
Commentary: For the avoidance of doubt, a Respondent Club’s failure to respond or failure to respond according to the
requirements above will be considered an admission of the breach.
16.4. League Decision
Where a Response is required as provided above, the League may:
16.4.1. Further investigate the potential breach;
16.4.2. Request additional information from the Club(s); and/or
16.4.3. Where it determines that the Respondent Club has breached these Guidelines, issue a decision; or
16.4.4. Where it determines that the Respondent Club did not breach these Guidelines, notify the relevant MLS
Clubs participating in the dispute that the matter is concluded and take no further action. As a result,
the Claimant Club must pay the full bond.
16.5. Statute of Limitations
A Claim must be filed within one (1) year from the later of: (A) the date the breach is asserted to have
occurred or (B) the date that the MLS Club was notified or made reasonably aware that the asserted
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 26

breach occurred (Statute of Limitations). Save for exceptional circumstances, an MLS Club is
precluded from bringing a Claim outside of the Statute of Limitations.
17. HOMEGROWN PLAYER LIST CONFLICT RESOLUTION
17.1. Players Who Appear on More Than One List – League Redress
If a player is included on more than one MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List, the League will determine,
on a case-by-case basis, whether to grant or revoke Homegrown Exclusivity based on the following
(non-exhaustive) factors:
a. Whether the player is registered to an MLS Club;
b. Whether the player was formerly registered to an MLS Club;
c. The player’s parent’s home address (and recent history of home addresses);
d. Whether an MLS Club engaged in conduct that is considered a potential breach of these Guidelines.
17.2. Players Who Appear on More Than One List – MLS Club Redress
Further to Section 17.1, if an MLS Club alleges that another MLS Club engaged in conduct that is
considered a potential breach of the Guidelines, the alleging MLS Club may:
a. Where applicable, include the player on its Homegrown List;
b. Where applicable, negotiate a settlement with the other MLS Club; or
c. File a Claim under the requirements set out in Section 16.
18. ACADEMY COMMITMENT LETTERS
The following provisions will apply if a player who signed an Academy Commitment Letter with an MLS
Club’s Academy ultimately does not register:
18.1.1. If the MLS Club unilaterally refuses or fails to register the player and/or to include the player on its
Homegrown Player List, the MLS Club will lose two (2) Homegrown Player List Slots for the next two (2)
Homegrown Player List Submission Dates.
18.1.2. The Academy Commitment Letter is not binding on the player. However, if the player unilaterally refuses
or fails to register with the MLS Club’s Academy and instead registers with a different MLS Club’s
Academy, then the MLS Club with which he signed an Academy Commitment Letter may protect its
rights by filing a claim.
18.1.3. If the player and the MLS Club’s Academy mutually agree to void the signed Academy Commitment
Letter, then the player and the MLS Club’s Academy must first notify the MLS Player Relations
Department at Homegrown.PlayerRelations@mlssoccer.com in writing no less than one week prior to
the opening of the next Homegrown Player List Submission Date. Following such notice, the League will
void the signed Academy Commitment Letter and it will have no further effect.
19. INVESTIGATIONS AND ENFORCEMENT
19.1. League Discretion
The League may investigate any allegation or suspicion of a breach of these Guidelines, whether
reported by an MLS Club or otherwise.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 27

19.2. MLS Club Document Production
Where the League decides to investigate, the League may require an MLS Club to produce documents
or correspondence within the MLS Club’s power, possession, custody and/or control.
19.3. MLS Commissioner’s Authority
Without prejudice to the enforcement mechanisms detailed in these Guidelines, the Roster and
Budget Guidelines or any League Rules, if following its investigation of an MLS Club the League
determines that the MLS Club breached these Guidelines, the matter may subsequently be referred
to the MLS Commissioner or his designee, who may impose penalties or sanctions under League Rules.
20. DECISIONS AND SANCTIONS
At any time, the League may issue a decision against an MLS Club in breach of these Guidelines (a
Decision). A Decision by the League is final. If a Decision includes a sanction, the sanction will be assessed
based on the severity of the breach and the number of breaches committed by the MLS Club in a
designated period, and may result in the following non-exhaustive list of sanctions:
a. A reprimand or warning regarding future conduct;
b. Public disclosure to all MLS Clubs;
c. Removal of Homegrown Player slots – The 35 and/or Plus 5;
d. An allocation penalty payable to another MLS Club; and/or
e. A financial penalty payable to the League, which must be paid within thirty (30) days of the date of
the Decision Letter sent to the MLS Club.
21. CONFIDENTIALITY AND PUBLIC DISCOURSE
20.1. Confidentiality Requirement
All investigations, Claims, Responses, and Decisions will be conducted on a private and confidential
basis between the League and the relevant MLS Club(s). However, the League may notify other MLS
Clubs of any Decision or findings.
20.2. No Public Comment
An MLS Club must not publicly comment on any compliance matter detailed in these Guidelines.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 28

[ACADEMY NAME]
ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF PLAYER OBLIGATIONS
Given your unique skill and ability, you have a choice as to which youth club you believe is the best fit for you going forward. As
a member of our academy team, you understand and acknowledge the below obligations and expectations with respect to your
membership in the [ACADEMY NAME].
1. Academy Mission and Player Acknowledgement
Our Academy is fully funded by, and is the professional development program of, the [CLUB NAME]. As such, the primary
mission of this Academy is to develop players who will sign with, and play for, our first team. Therefore, you should only sign
this Acknowledgement below if you understand and acknowledge the following:
• As part of your development, we will provide you with periodic assessments of your progress, including areas
of improvement.
• If, as part of that assessment process, we determine that you have the ability to compete as a professional player,
we will offer you an employment agreement with the [CLUB NAME] (through Major League Soccer).
• If you receive any communications from a non-MLS club, you will notify [ACADEMY NAME] immediately.
If you receive an offer to play professional soccer from any non-MLS club, you will immediately notify
[ACADEMY NAME], and in such case Major League Soccer will have up to fourteen (14) calendar days to
offer you an employment agreement.
• If you enter an agreement to play professional soccer with a non-MLS professional soccer club outside of the
U.S. and Canada, you understand and acknowledge that the [CLUB NAME] will be compensated for your
development through the FIFA Clearing House. The amount of such obligation will be determined based on
the FIFA Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players. If you subsequently transfer internationally
between professional clubs, the [CLUB NAME] will also receive solidarity payments through the FIFA
Clearing House under the FIFA Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players.
2. Academy Program
Our Academy Program provides a unique environment for the most gifted youth players to advance their development goals. Our
Academy program is fully funded by us, and therefore there is no cost to you to participate. This is unique in [THE UNITED
STATES / CANADA], as most youth programs require a significant investment by the player (and his family) to participate.
The Academy offers elite youth players advanced training and competitive opportunities, including playing matches against top
international clubs. Our Academy staff includes some of the most well-respected coaches in [THE UNITED STATES /
CANADA], using teaching methodologies consistent with top academies around the world and working alongside MLS sports
science and medical experts at a state-of-the-art training facility.
Our program provides at no cost to you:
• [Regular training;
• Top tier coaching staff;
• MLS NEXT participation;
• Competitive games against top international clubs;
• World class training facilities;
• Access to a dedicated team of medical and sport science experts including a strength and conditioning specialist, a
nutritionist and a sports psychologist, as well as a team of medical practitioners who will provide comprehensive support
for the treatment of injuries;
• Media relations training;
• Transportation to and from team events and reimbursement of expenses;
• Professional training curriculum tailored specifically to you;
• Apparel; and
• Equipment.]
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 29

You and your parents understand and acknowledge that the Academy will incur significant costs and will make significant
monetary investments in your development.
3. NCAA Eligibility
Unless and until you and the [CLUB NAME] agree to enter a professional contract, it is the intention of you, your Parent or
Guardian, and the [CLUB NAME] that your National Collegiate Athletic Association (“NCAA”) eligibility be preserved and
nothing in this Acknowledgement should be construed in a manner that could compromise your NCAA eligibility. In addition,
any entitlement to any expense, reimbursement or other payment by the [CLUB NAME] to you will only be made if such payment:
(i) was previously approved by the [CLUB NAME]; (ii) was actually and necessarily incurred by the Player; and (iii) does not
compromise your NCAA eligibility.

EXHIBIT 2
HOMEGROWN INFORMATION SHEET
This document summarizes the 2025 MLS Player Development Guidelines that administer MLS Club player
development programs (and, as applicable, League competition) and answers many frequently asked questions.
Please be advised that the League reserves the right to modify and/or create exceptions to the MLS Player
Development Guidelines at any time, in its sole and absolute discretion.
Homegrown Player Signings
1. A player is eligible to be signed by MLS as a Homegrown Player if the MLS Club establishes and maintains:
a. Homegrown Priority after the completion of at least twelve (12) continuous months registered with an
MLS Club’s Academy or MLS Club’s NEXT Pro team, which must fall between January 31 of the year
in which the player turns fourteen (14) and the year in which he turns eighteen (18); and
b. Homegrown Exclusivity by properly and accurately listing the player on its Homegrown Player List.
2. MLS Club’s may retain the right to sign eligible former Academy Players who have been offered a
professional contract, gone to college, or signed a professional contract with the MLS Club in MLS NEXT
Pro.
3. If an Academy Player who is not included on an MLS Club’s Homegrown Player List is recruited and
chooses to register with another MLS Club, the new MLS Club may be required to provide specified
compensation to the former MLS Club. MLS Clubs may negotiate varying terms.
Should you have any questions about this Homegrown Information Sheet or the MLS Player Development
Guidelines, please contact Major League Soccer or the Major League Soccer Players Association as follows:

EXHIBIT 5
FROM: MLS Player Relations
RE: Training Compensation – Updated MLS Guidance
The League Office has recently reviewed its existing guidance to MLS Clubs entitled to Training Compensation
under the FIFA Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players (the “FIFA RSTP”) for players trained in their
Academies.
To ensure our player development efforts reflect the current FIFA Training Compensation and Solidarity landscape
and the change in process due to the implementation of the FIFA Clearing House, League Office guidance to MLS
Clubs going forward is the following:
• Where an MLS Club is entitled to Training Compensation under the FIFA RSTP for a player trained in its
Academy (and/or with its MLS NEXT Pro team) who did not sign an MLS contract, the MLS Club may
receive such compensation regardless of whether it offered the player an MLS contract.
• If an MLS player seeks to join a non-MLS club as an “out-of-contract” player following termination of his
MLS contract, the MLS Club must waive Training Compensation if so requested by the player’s new club.
• MLS Academies must continue to require all players and their parent(s)/guardian(s) to sign the
Acknowledgment of Player Obligations Memo each year as a condition of their acceptance into the
Academy.
Below, we provide further detail on the points above. Please note that this guidance only applies to claims for
training compensation under the FIFA RSTP and does not apply to MLS Right of First Refusal rules or to claims
for solidarity payments under the FIFA RSTP.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 35

Not Required to Offer an MLS Contract to Receive Training Compensation. MLS is rescinding its prior
guidance requiring MLS/an MLS Club to offer an MLS contract to a player to receive Training Compensation for
his development. Subject to the exception detailed below, this generally means an MLS Club may claim training
compensation for its former Academy (and/or MLS NEXT Pro) player who was offered: (i) an MLS contract, (ii)
an MLS NEXT Pro contract, or (iii) no contract at all.
Must Waive Training Compensation for MLS Players if Requested. Under Section 15.4 of the MLS Collective
Bargaining Agreement, a former MLS player must be free to sign a contract with any professional non-MLS club
without MLS or an MLS Club requiring payment of Training Compensation. If requested by the player’s new club,
MLS and MLS Clubs must agree to waive Training Compensation and provide the necessary documentation for
submission to the FIFA Clearing House.
This waiver requirement does not apply to players who only signed an MLS NEXT Pro contract. Clubs should act
reasonably given the circumstances, reviewing the amount of Training Compensation at issue for a particular player
and consider waiving it if requested.
Academy Players Must Sign Acknowledgment of Player Obligations Memo Each Year. MLS Club Academies
must continue to require all players and their parent(s)/guardian(s) to sign the Acknowledgment of Player
Obligations Memo each year as a condition of their acceptance into the Academy. As a reminder, the
Acknowledgment of Player Obligations Memo details, among other things, the following:
• The player acknowledges that if he signs a professional contract with a non-MLS club outside of the U.S.
or Canada, then the MLS Academy will be compensated for his development through the FIFA Clearing
House, calculated according to the FIFA RSTP.
• The player also acknowledges that future international transfers of the player may result in the MLS
Academy receiving Solidarity payments through the FIFA Clearing House, calculated according to the
FIFA RSTP.
• The player must notify his MLS Academy if he receives a communication from a non-MLS club.
• If the player receives an offer from a non-MLS club, the player must notify his MLS Academy, and MLS
will have up to fourteen (14) calendar days to offer him an employment agreement.
MLS PLAYER DEVELOPMENT GUIDELINES – PAGE 36
